text.title: php-phpunit-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for php-phpunit-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: assertJsonStructure(['data' => ['email', 'name']]) used instead of assertJsonPath for value verification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: assertJsonStructure confirms keys exist but passes when values are null, 0, or wrong types. It provides almost no behavioral signal. Use assertJsonPath('data.email', $expected) to verify the actual value.

failure-mode.description: Calling Mail::fake(), Queue::fake(), or Event::fake() after the action under test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fakes intercept calls only from the moment they are set. If the action runs first, the real implementation fires and the fake captures nothing. The assertion then passes on an empty fake — a silent false positive.

failure-mode.description: Using RefreshDatabase in a Unit test: trait drops and re-migrates the schema for every test method, making fast logic tests 100x slower.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unit tests should not touch the database. Use Mockery to mock repositories. Reserve RefreshDatabase for Feature tests that exercise the HTTP + DB path together.

failure-mode.description: Mixing Pest function syntax (it(), describe()) and PHPUnit class syntax in the same file.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pest and PHPUnit parsers are separate; mixing syntax within one file causes parse errors or unexpected test discovery. Pick one syntax per project and enforce via code review.

failure-mode.description: Missing RefreshDatabase on Feature tests that write to the DB, causing state pollution across test methods run in random order.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PHPUnit randomizes method execution order by default when executionOrder="random" is set. A test that depends on clean state fails intermittently when another test has left records behind.

failure-mode.description: Mail::fake() on a mail that is dispatched via a queued job: the fake captures sync sends only; the queued job runs asynchronously and the assertion sees nothing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For queued mail, assert the job was pushed (Queue::assertPushed) or use Notification::fake() if the mail goes via a Notification channel. Mail::fake() only intercepts direct Mail::send() calls.

failure-mode.description: RefreshDatabase is per-test reset — slow on large schemas. DatabaseTransactions is faster but breaks for code that opens its own transactions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Understand the tradeoff: RefreshDatabase guarantees isolation but takes time; DatabaseTransactions is faster but incompatible with code that starts its own transactions. Test the combination and measure suite time.

failure-mode.description: Using expectsExceptionMessage with unstable strings like timestamps or generated IDs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: expectsExceptionMessage matches substring — if the exception message includes a timestamp or ID, the test becomes flaky. Use expectExceptionMessageMatches with a regex instead for stability.

failure-mode.description: LLM places fake() calls AFTER the action — the assertion then sees an empty fake, false positives.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hard rule: fakes go at the top of the test method, before the act. Calling fake() after the action captures nothing.

failure-mode.description: Mail::fake() plus Mail::send inside a queued job — fake captures only sync sends.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For queued mail, use Notification::fake() or Queue::fake() and assert the job was pushed instead. Mail::fake() only intercepts direct Mail::send() calls, not jobs.

failure-mode.description: Coverage targets the LLM proposes (often 100%) lead to brittle tests. Pick semantic coverage: every public method has at least one happy + one failure path.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Aiming for 100% coverage often means testing implementation details instead of behavior. Semantic coverage (happy + failure paths for public methods) is more maintainable and catches regressions better.
